content: The language education definition of reflection as a metacognitive vocabulary learning strategy can be borrowed to improve LLM self-reflection mechanisms, by framing agent self-reflection as iterative 'vocabulary building' for stored problem-solving patterns in the Reflexion agent's dynamic memory. Just as students learn to categorize and refine their understanding of new words through repeated reflection, LLM agents can refine their abstract problem-solving heuristics by 'lexicalizing' successful and failed reasoning trajectories into retrievable memory entries.
